# Edtools Labs

> Open-source AI STEAM tutor that teaches kinematics with voice, an Excalidraw-style canvas and the phone's tilt sensors. Bilingual EN/ES. Built with Claude Opus 4.7.

## Product

- AI tutor for STEAM, focused on physics and kinematics (MRU, MRUA, free fall, velocity vs speed).
- Three teaching channels in a single flow:
  - Voice (Web Speech API and Kokoro TTS) for narration in sync with the canvas.
  - Excalidraw-style canvas for diagrams, vectors and conceptual graphs.
  - Smartphone tilt sensors (Device Orientation API) for hands-on mini-labs.
- Inline mini-labs follow a Predict, Measure, Reflect cycle and return automatically to the lesson with handoff context.
- Bilingual EN/ES across UI, voice and prompts. Languages can be switched without losing progress.
- Demo mode runs the full UI without an API key for evaluation and prototyping.
- Progress is stored in localStorage and surfaced to the model so suggestions evolve over time.

## Open source

- License: GPL-3.0
- Repository: https://github.com/soyandresalcedo/Edtools-labs-landing
- Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Anthropic Claude.

## How to use

- Open the live app at https://app.labs.edtools.co (marketing site: https://labs.edtools.co).
- Self-host by cloning the repo and configuring `.env.local`, or enabling demo mode for a no-key evaluation.
- Mobile use is recommended over HTTPS so tilt sensors are available; a desktop slider fallback exists.

## Pages

- Landing: https://labs.edtools.co
- GitHub: https://github.com/soyandresalcedo/Edtools-labs-landing
